title: Fluent delegation chain builder API

artifacts.description: TypeScript interfaces: ScopeFlags, DelegationHop, DelegationToken (portable JSON), ChainBuilderConfig, and BitmaskInvariant registry

Scope bit encoder: maps human-readable flags ({canRead, canWrite, financialLimit}) to uint bitmask; enforces implication invariants (e.g. bit 4 requires bits 2+3) at encode time with descriptive Error throws
Poseidon-based commitment utilities: computeCredentialCommitment(identity, salt) and computeScopeCommitment(bitmask, nonce) — thin wrappers over circomlibjs matching circuit expectations

Core fluent builder: DelegationChain.from(agentIdentity) → HopBuilder; HopBuilder.narrow(ScopeFlags) validates cumulative narrowing (child ⊆ parent), HopBuilder.to(delegateeIdentity).expiresIn(duration) adds hop; .addHop() enables chaining up to 3 hops; .build() threads previousScopeCommitment automatically and serializes to DelegationToken JSON
Public re-exports and convenience factory for the builder package
Canonical bitmask layout table, implication invariant matrix, multi-hop threading algorithm, JSON token schema, and error catalog
Unit tests: all flag combinations, every invariant violation path, edge cases (zero scope, full scope), round-trip encode/decode
Integration tests: single-hop happy path, 2-hop and 3-hop chains, 4-hop rejection, scope-widening rejection, expired hop, JSON token round-trip, circuit witness compatibility smoke test
Developer guide: quick-start examples, bitmask flag reference, error messages and remediation, circuit input mapping, token verification pattern

steps: Define ScopeFlags interface and canonical 8-bit bitmask layout in types.ts; document which bit positions map to canRead, canWrite, financialLimit tiers, and admin

Build implication invariant registry in ScopeBitmask.ts: static table mapping each bit to its prerequisite bits; encode(flags) throws BitmaskInvariantError with 'bit N requires bits X,Y' message before returning BigInt

Implement CredentialCommitment.ts: wrap circomlibjs Poseidon to compute identity commitment (hash of pubkey + salt) and scope commitment (hash of bitmask + nonce); export deterministic helpers used by both builder and circuit prover
Implement DelegationChain.from(agentIdentity): validates identity shape, initializes hop list, returns a new HopBuilder bound to hop index 0

Implement HopBuilder.narrow(ScopeFlags): calls ScopeBitmask.encode() for invariant check, then verifies new bitmask is a bitwise subset of parent hop's bitmask (throws ScopeExpansionError if not), stores encoded scope
Implement HopBuilder.to(delegateeIdentity).expiresIn(duration): parses duration string to absolute Unix timestamp, validates delegatee identity, finalizes current hop record
Implement HopBuilder.addHop(): enforces MAX_HOPS=3 limit (throws ChainLimitError), returns new HopBuilder for next hop with parent scope reference set

Implement HopBuilder.build(): iterates hop list, computes each hop's credentialCommitment and scopeCommitment, threads previousScopeCommitment = scopeCommitment[i-1] (null for hop 0), packs all fields into DelegationToken JSON with a version field and chain metadata

Add toCircuitInputs() method on DelegationToken: flattens token into the flat BigInt arrays expected by the Delegation circuit (scopeBitmasks[], credentialCommitments[], previousScopeCommitments[], expirations[]) with zero-padding to 3-hop length
Write ScopeBitmask unit tests covering all invariant paths and every valid combination; use property-based approach for exhaustive bit pair coverage

Write DelegationChainBuilder integration tests: happy-path 1/2/3-hop chains, 4-hop rejection, scope widening rejection, expiresIn parsing, toCircuitInputs() shape validation, and JSON round-trip deserialization
Write docs/delegation-chain-spec.md: bitmask table, invariant matrix, threading algorithm prose, JSON token schema (JSON Schema format), error catalog
Write docs/delegation-chain-builder.md: quick-start code example matching the proposal snippet, flag reference table, error remediation guide, circuit integration pattern
dependencies: Delegation circuit (existing) — defines bitmask layout, commitment hash function, and 3-hop wire structure
circomlibjs — Poseidon hash matching in-circuit computation
snarkjs — witness generation for toCircuitInputs() smoke test
zod — runtime validation of identity shapes and token deserialization
TypeScript 5.x — satisfies-based exhaustive invariant checks at compile time
